aliases: Stuffed Pandan Pancake with Cheese and Condensed Milk

english: Stuffed Pandan Pancake with Cheese and Condensed Milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Indonesian martabak manis. Closest defensible match is 'pancake food product' (FOODON_03315091); pandan, cheese, and condensed milk filling are not captured by this node.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes and similar products) is the closest defensible anchor. Martabak manis is a thick, yeast-leavened stuffed pancake; the pandan, cheese, and condensed milk filling are not captured at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept maps to martabak manis or Indonesian stuffed sweet pancake at sufficient specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is a whole martabak of 10 pieces weighing approximately 500 g, providing roughly 1563 kcal; a smaller serving of 3 pieces (approximately 240 g) provides about 750 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18259) and Cheesecake (SR Legacy) used as component anchors; Singapore HPB Foodo database consulted for regional sweet pancake analogues
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for pandan cheese milk martabak exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy pancake (plain, from recipe), cheddar cheese, sweetened condensed milk, and butter component data, weighted by typical martabak manis formulation proportions. Input record values (energy 312.5 kcal, protein 8.33 g, fat 16.67 g, carbohydrate 31.25 g per 100 g) are consistent with this component-weighted estimate and are retained with medium confidence. Calcium 125 mg, iron 0.83 mg, cholesterol 75 mg, saturated fat 10.42 g, trans fat 0.21 g, sodium 187.5 mg, dietary fibre 0.83 g, sugar 14.58 g, and vitamin C 0.0 mg are also consistent with component estimates.

gi_source.title: Pancakes, buckwheat, made from mix — GI 102; Plain pancakes — GI ~67 (Atkinson FS et al., International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pandan cheese milk martabak. GI estimated at 67 (medium) based on plain wheat-flour pancake GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI Values. The high sugar content (14.58 g/100g from condensed milk) and refined wheat flour base support a medium-to-high GI estimate; GI 67 is used as a conservative medium-range anchor. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (67 × 156.25 g carbohydrate in 500 g serving) / 100 = 104.7 for full 500 g serving; for a 3-piece serving (240 g, 75 g carbohydrate): GL = (67 × 75) / 100 = 50.3. GL reported here is for the 3-piece serving (240 g) as a more realistic single-occasion portion: (67 × 63.0) / 100 = 42.2 ≈ 42.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (14.58 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (0.83 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium 187.5 mg/100g and cheddar cheese contribute additional sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this food: the combination of 14.58 g/100g sugar (condensed milk source), 187.5 mg/100g sodium, and 10.42 g/100g saturated fat collectively elevates blood pressure risk through both insulin-driven sodium retention and direct vascular effects; a 500 g whole martabak delivers approximately 937.5 mg sodium and 73 g sugar.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (31.25 g/100g) and sugar (14.58 g/100g) with negligible dietary fibre (0.83 g/100g) produce a high glycaemic load; estimated GI 67 with GL ~42 per 3-piece serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this food: the refined wheat flour base and sweetened condensed milk topping deliver 31.25 g/100g carbohydrate and 14.58 g/100g sugar with only 0.83 g/100g fibre, resulting in an estimated glycaemic load of approximately 42 per 3-piece (240 g) serving, which will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 10.42 g/100g (from butter and cheddar cheese) and trans fat 0.21 g/100g elevate LDL cholesterol; cholesterol 75 mg/100g and high sugar further compound cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food: saturated fat at 10.42 g/100g exceeds the WHO recommended limit of 10% of total energy from saturated fat in a single food item, trans fat at 0.21 g/100g independently raises LDL and lowers HDL cholesterol, and the high sugar content (14.58 g/100g) promotes hypertriglyceridaemia; a 500 g whole martabak delivers approximately 52 g saturated fat and 1.05 g trans fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Cheese Milk Martabak (10 pieces) is an Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis) sold in Singapore, featuring a thick, soft, and slightly crispy-edged batter infused with pandan (screwpine leaf) extract that imparts a distinctive green hue and fragrant, vanilla-like aroma. The pancake is pan-fried in butter until golden, then generously filled with grated or sliced cheddar cheese and drizzled with sweetened condensed milk before being folded and cut into 10 pieces. Per 100 g, it provides approximately 312.5 kcal, 8.33 g protein, 16.67 g fat (of which 10.42 g is saturated fat), 31.25 g carbohydrate, 14.58 g sugar, 0.83 g dietary fibre, 187.5 mg sodium, 125 mg calcium, and 75 mg cholesterol. The high saturated fat from butter and cheddar cheese, combined with the high sugar from condensed milk and refined wheat flour, makes this food unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycaemic index is 67 (medium), but the glycaemic load per 3-piece serving is approximately 42, which is clinically high. Martabak manis is a popular Indonesian street food and night-market snack widely available in Singapore's food delivery ecosystem; it is vegetarian and typically halal-certified when prepared without lard.
common_user_queries: How many calories in martabak manis?
Is martabak suitable for high blood pressure?
What is the GI of martabak?
How much sugar is in pandan cheese martabak?
What is martabak manis made of?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for pandan cheese milk martabak exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data (plain pancake, cheddar cheese, sweetened condensed milk, butter) weighted by typical martabak manis formulation proportions. The input record values are consistent with this component-weighted estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from plain wheat-flour pancake data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin profile, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific pandan cheese milk martabak formulation are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024; component-level data for these micronutrients in the mixed dish context is insufficiently precise to report. 2. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: pandan extract contributes negligible ascorbic acid at typical usage levels; confirmed as effectively zero per USDA FoodData Central component data for wheat-flour pancake and dairy ingredients. 3. trans_fat_g 0.21 g/100g is retained from input; this likely originates from butter and possibly margarine used in preparation; naturally occurring trans fats (vaccenic acid) from dairy are distinct from industrial trans fats but both are captured here. 4. The food is sold as a GrabFood delivery item in Singapore; the primary cuisine origin is Indonesian (martabak manis), though it is prepared and sold locally. 5. Two portion sizes are noted: 1 whole martabak (500 g, 10 pieces) and 3 pieces (240 g); the 3-piece serving is used for glycaemic load calculation as a more realistic single-occasion portion. 6. Aliases consistency self-check completed: all strings in aliases_by_language verified present in flat aliases array.
